# ae (@aeroapp/ae) — compact API for AI agents

Attribute-based DOM reactivity, 3.2 KB min+gzip, zero deps, ES module.
HTML is the source of truth: write real markup, attach behavior by name.
No virtual DOM, no hydration, no build step, no template syntax.

```html
<button data-ae="save">Save</button>
<span data-ae="status"></span>
<script type="module">
  import { ae } from '@aeroapp/ae';
  const saves = ae.signal(0);
  ae('status').text(() => `saved ${saves.value}×`);
  ae('save').press(() => saves.value++);
</script>
```

Core model: `ae(name)` returns a cached, LIVE handle for every element with
`data-ae="name"` — present or added later (one shared MutationObserver binds
new elements and fully disposes removed ones: effects, listeners, cleanups).
Never manually re-bind or unbind.

## Signals

- `ae.signal(v)` → `{ value }`. Writes batch per microtask; `Object.is`-equal
  writes are no-ops. Three writes in one tick → one re-render.
- `ae.computed(fn)` → read-only `{ value }`; lazy; notifies only when the
  computed value actually changes.
- `ae.effect(fn)` → auto-tracked side effect, runs immediately, returns
  dispose function.
- `ae.isSignal(v)` → true for signals/computeds.
- `ae.settled()` → Promise; resolves when writes, list stamping, and mounts
  have drained. Use in tests instead of setTimeout.

## Handle methods (all chain, callbacks get the element first)

- `.render(fn)` — run `fn(el)` per element, auto-tracking signal reads;
  re-runs on change and for elements added later.
- `.mount(fn)` — setup once per element; return a function to run as cleanup
  on removal.
- `.scope(fn)` — per-root setup that creates SCOPED handles (see below).
- `.press(fn)` — activation: click, plus Enter/Space only for elements the
  browser doesn't natively activate (no double-fire on real buttons).
- `.hover(enter, leave?)` — pointerenter/pointerleave.
- `.on(type, fn, opts?)` — any DOM event, per-element listener (non-bubbling
  events work). Handler signature: `(el, event)`.
- `.text(v)` `.cls(name, on?)` `.attr(name, v)` `.show(v)` — sugar; `v` may be
  a plain value (applied once), a signal, or a function `(el) => value`
  (both reactive). `.attr`: null/undefined/false removes, true sets empty.
- `.input(signal)` — two-way form binding typed by field: text/textarea/select
  ↔ Signal<string>, checkbox ↔ Signal<boolean>, number/range ↔ Signal<number>
  via valueAsNumber (empty number field reads NaN — guard for it). Writes are
  equality-guarded (caret never moves). Multiple fields on one signal mirror
  each other. Radio groups / multi-select: use `.on` instead.
- `.list(items, render, key?)` — keyed list stamping from the container's
  native `<template>` (exactly one root element). `items`: signal/computed/
  function of an array. `render(el, item, index)` runs in its own effect.
  `key` defaults to item identity — pass `(item) => item.id`. Reorders MOVE
  nodes (no remount); unchanged items don't re-render; removed items dispose.
- `.els` — current matching elements (plain array). `.each(fn)` — one-shot
  iteration, not reactive.

## Lists: recovering the item in handlers

`ae.itemOf(el)` → the `.list` item for the stamped node containing `el`
(nearest stamped ancestor; nested lists resolve innermost). No key stamping
in the DOM needed:

```js
ae('todos').list(todos, (li, todo) => {
  ae.parts(li).title.textContent = todo.text;   // ae.parts: named descendants
}, (t) => t.id);
ae('remove').press((btn) => {
  const todo = ae.itemOf(btn);
  todos.value = todos.value.filter((t) => t.id !== todo.id);
});
```

`ae.parts(root)` → `{ name: element }` for `data-ae` descendants of a stamped
node (cached; static structure only).

## Scoped handles — same names, private wiring

`ae(name, root)` scopes a handle to descendants of `root`. For roots that can
come and go (anything stamped by `.list`), create scoped handles inside
`.scope`, which retires them on unmount so a remount rebuilds cleanly:

```js
ae('column').scope((colEl) => {
  const colId = ae.itemOf(colEl).id;
  const mine = ae.computed(() => cards.value.filter((c) => c.col === colId));
  ae('cards', colEl).list(mine, renderCard, (c) => c.id);
  ae('del', colEl).press((btn) => remove(ae.itemOf(btn).id));
});
```

RULES: never create scoped handles inside a `.list` render callback (it
re-runs → duplicate bindings); set up each root exactly once (that is what
`.scope` guarantees).

## Animation

`ae.transition(fn)` — runs `fn` (signal writes) inside a View Transition;
enters/exits/reorders animate via CSS `::view-transition-*`. Elements with a
unique `style.viewTransitionName` MORPH, even across two `.list` containers.
Unsupported browsers: `fn` just runs, returns undefined. Style
`prefers-reduced-motion` yourself.

## Guarantees

Batching (one effect run per flush); absolute disposal (a disposed effect
never runs again, even if queued); net-state lifecycle (moves don't remount,
a→b→a renames are no-ops); fault isolation (one throwing binding never breaks
the rest); runaway guard (self-triggering effects trip a breaker after 100
cycles instead of hanging).

## Gotchas

- Writes flush on the NEXT microtask — `await ae.settled()` before asserting
  on the DOM.
- An item moved BETWEEN two `.list` containers is a remove + fresh stamp (new
  node): transient DOM state (focus, scroll) doesn't travel. Use
  `ae.transition` + `view-transition-name` to visually morph it.
- `ae.parts` root itself is not a part; first match wins on duplicates.
- Duplicate `.list` keys log an error and fall back to fresh nodes.

Full prose spec: API.md in the repo (github.com/nft/ae).
